steps.title: Track the passage focus
steps.content: The text first identifies Dubautia menziesii as part of the silversword alliance, a plant family found only on the Hawaiian Islands. It then widens to the whole alliance, describing the unusually broad range of plant forms, including vines, shrubs, and trees.

steps.title: Connect origin to variety
steps.content: The second half explains how that variety developed: all members of the alliance descended from one ancestral tarweed that arrived about 5 million years ago. Its descendants became distinct species as they adapted to the different habitats on the islands.

steps.title: Choose the complete purpose
steps.content: Choice B is too narrow because the text does not describe a specific habitat for Dubautia menziesii. Choice C wrongly expands the claim to all Hawaiian plants, and Choice D falsely says all plants on the islands are in the alliance. The remaining choice captures both the description of the alliance and the explanation for its diversity. The answer is A.
